sections.heading: Intent
sections.content:
The GitHub Actions gate is a read-only check that runs against the existing `cortex.db` on every PR. It does not rebuild the index — it inspects the verification state of every node and fails the check if any nodes do not meet the configured strictness level.

The primary output of a failed check is an **impact report**: a structured summary of every non-clean node, what changed, and what still needs to be verified. This gives reviewers immediate context on what the PR affected and what sign-off is still required.

sections.heading: Strictness Levels
sections.content:
The action is configurable with a strictness level that controls what counts as acceptable:

| Level | Description |
|---|---|
| `stale` | Fails if any node is DESC_STALE or STRUCTURAL_DRIFT |
| `agent` | Fails if any node is not at minimum AGENT_VERIFIED |
| `human` | Fails if any node is not HUMAN_VERIFIED — agent verification alone is insufficient |

Strictness level is set per-repo in the workflow YAML. Teams can choose the appropriate gate for their trust model.

sections.heading: Impact Report
sections.content:
When the gate runs, it produces an impact report: a structured markdown document summarizing all changes since the reference point. The report is ordered by urgency — unverified nodes first (action required), then agent-verified (needs human review), then human-verified (done), then new nodes and test results.

The report is generated by `cortex report`, which performs a pure SQL read over `node_history`, `node_verification`, and `test_results`. No LLM involved — deterministic and fast. The same data is served by `GET /api/impact` for the viz "Generate Report" button, so developers can view the same information interactively in the viz (with graph, diff, and detail panel available for drill-down).

**Report sections:**
1. **Summary** — counts at a glance: total changed, verified (agent/human), unverified, tests pass/fail
2. **Unverified** — nodes still stale with no verification: node ID, change type, current staleness, last verified date
3. **Agent-verified** — nodes an agent marked clean: node ID, change type, agent identity, reason text
4. **Human-verified** — nodes a human signed off on: node ID, who, reason
5. **New nodes** — first discovered in this window (INITIAL change type): node ID, location
6. **Test results** — per-node test health for changed nodes: test ID, status, duration

The report is uploaded as a PR artifact and optionally posted as a PR comment. The viz "since" filter shows the same data interactively — the developer can click any node to see the diff, review the docs, and verify directly from the viz.

sections.heading: Resolution Paths
sections.content:
When the gate fails, there are two paths to resolution:

**Human resolution:** The reviewer opens cortex viz, reviews the impact report and affected nodes, and manually marks nodes clean. The PR check re-runs against the updated `cortex.db`.

**Agent resolution:** The PEV agent is triggered by the failed check, updates stale documentation, records decisions, and marks nodes verified at the appropriate level. If strictness is `human`, a human must still review and promote the agent's work to HUMAN_VERIFIED via cortex viz before the gate passes.

In both cases the impact report in cortex viz is the review surface — the human does not need to dig through diffs manually.

sections.heading: Dependencies (Not Yet Implemented)
sections.content:
| Dependency | Status |
|---|---|
| `node_verification` table in `cortex.db` | Done |
| `--fail-on` flag on `cortex check` | Done |
| `verified_by` field (agent vs human distinction) | Done |
| `code_hash_at` / `desc_hash_at` verification snapshots | Done |
| `git_sha` populated in `node_history` during builds | Done |
| `cortex report` command (impact report generation) | Done |
| `cortex_report` MCP tool | Done |
| Batch `cortex_mark_clean` | Done |
| GitHub Actions workflow YAML | Not started |
| Linting / formatting enforcement (ruff) | Not started |

The gate can be implemented now — all upstream dependencies are complete. The remaining work is the GitHub Actions workflow YAML and linting/formatting setup.
